ci_support_linux_64_c_stdlib_version2.17:
c_compiler:
- gcc
c_compiler_version:
- '14'
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cuda_compiler:
- cuda-nvcc
cuda_compiler_version:
- '12.9'
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
openssl:
- '3.5'
target_platform:
- linux-64
zip_keys:
- - c_compiler_version
  - cxx_compiler_version
  - c_stdlib_version
  - cuda_compiler_version
zlib:
- '1'

about.description:
CV-CUDA is an open-source library of GPU-accelerated computer vision algorithms designed
for speed and scalability. It delivers high-throughput, low-latency image/video
processing for AI pipelines across NVIDIA cloud, desktop, and edge platforms. CV-CUDA is
built for performance and works seamlessly with C/C++ and Python Image and AI
frameworks.

This package provides the C/C++ shared libraries and their headers.
about.summary: An open-source, GPU accelerated library for cloud-scale image processing and computer vision

about.description:
CV-CUDA is an open-source library of GPU-accelerated computer vision algorithms designed
for speed and scalability. It delivers high-throughput, low-latency image/video
processing for AI pipelines across NVIDIA cloud, desktop, and edge platforms. CV-CUDA is
built for performance and works seamlessly with C/C++ and Python Image and AI
frameworks.

This package is the runtime library only. Developers should install libcvcuda-dev instead.
about.summary: An open-source, GPU accelerated library for cloud-scale image processing and computer vision
C++ system tests for CV-CUDA operators

name: libcvcuda-split
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = "0.16.0" %}

package:
  name: libcvcuda-split
  version: {{ version }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/CVCUDA/CV-CUDA/archive/refs/tags/v{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 49d74f5958d9ce613c12ed12019a4e69f547bb53ed61658e65a4b3972446661a
  patches:
    - patches/0001-fix-libdir.patch
    - patches/0002-use-dynamic-cuda-libs.patch
    - patches/0003-use-sysroot-libs-for-glibc-compat-stubs.patch
    - patches/0004-use-system-gtest.patch
    - patches/0005-use-dynamic-openssl-zlib-in-tests.patch
    - patches/0006-find-gtest-in-tests.patch
    - patches/0007-exclude-cuosd-tests.patch

build:
  number: 1
  skip: True  # [not linux or cuda_compiler_version == "None"]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ compiler('c') }}
    - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - {{ compiler('cuda') }}
    - {{ stdlib('c') }}
    - arm-variant * {{ arm_variant_type }}  # [linux and aarch64 and cuda_compiler_version != "None"]
    - cmake >=3.20.1
    - ninja
  host:
    - cuda-version {{ cuda_compiler_version }}
    - cuda-cudart-dev
    - libcublas-dev
    - libcurand-dev
    - libcusolver-dev
    - gtest
    - openssl
    - zlib

outputs:
  - name: libcvcuda
    build:
      string: cuda{{ cuda_compiler_version | replace('.', '') }}_h{{ PKG_HASH }}_{{ PKG_BUILDNUM }}
      ignore_run_exports_from:
        - cuda-cudart-dev
        - libcusolver-dev
      ignore_run_exports:
        - cuda-version
        - libstdcxx
    script: install_lib.sh
    requirements:
      build:
        - {{ compiler('c') }}
        - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - {{ compiler('cuda') }}
        - {{ stdlib('c') }}
        - arm-variant * {{ arm_variant_type }}  # [linux and aarch64 and cuda_compiler_version != "None"]
        - cmake >=3.20.1
      host:
        - cuda-version {{ cuda_compiler_version }}
        - cuda-cudart-dev
        - libcusolver-dev
      run:
        - {{ pin_compatible('cuda-version', max_pin='x', min_pin='x') }}
        - cuda-version >=12.2
        - cuda-cudart
        - libcusolver
    test:
      commands:
        - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libcvcuda.so.0
        - test -f $PREFIX/lib/libnvcv_types.so.0
    about:
      home: https://github.com/CVCUDA/CV-CUDA
      summary: 'An open-source, GPU accelerated library for cloud-scale image processing and computer vision'
      description: |
        CV-CUDA is an open-source library of GPU-accelerated computer vision algorithms designed
        for speed and scalability. It delivers high-throughput, low-latency image/video
        processing for AI pipelines across NVIDIA cloud, desktop, and edge platforms. CV-CUDA is
        built for performance and works seamlessly with C/C++ and Python Image and AI
        frameworks.

        This package is the runtime library only. Developers should install libcvcuda-dev instead.
      license: Apache-2.0 and MIT
      license_file:
        - LICENSE.md
        - THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES.md
      doc_url: https://cvcuda.github.io/CV-CUDA/
      dev_url: https://github.com/CVCUDA/CV-CUDA

  - name: libcvcuda-dev
    build:
      string: cuda{{ cuda_compiler_version | replace('.', '') }}_h{{ PKG_HASH }}_{{ PKG_BUILDNUM }}
      run_exports:
        - {{ pin_subpackage('libcvcuda', max_pin='x.x') }}
      ignore_run_exports:
        - cuda-version
    script: install_dev.sh
    requirements:
      build:
        - arm-variant * {{ arm_variant_type }}  # [linux and aarch64 and cuda_compiler_version != "None"]
        - cmake >=3.20.1
      host:
        - cuda-version {{ cuda_compiler_version }}
      run:
        - {{ pin_compatible('cuda-version', max_pin='x', min_pin='x') }}
        - {{ pin_subpackage('libcvcuda', exact=True) }}
    test:
      commands:
        - test -f $PREFIX/include/cvcuda/IOperator.hpp
        - test -f $PREFIX/include/nvcv/Version.h
        - test -f $PREFIX/lib/cmake/cvcuda/cvcuda-config.cmake

  - name: libcvcuda-tests
    build:
      string: cuda{{ cuda_compiler_version | replace('.', '') }}_h{{ PKG_HASH }}_{{ PKG_BUILDNUM }}
      ignore_run_exports:
        - cuda-version
      ignore_run_exports_from:
        - cuda-cudart-dev
      missing_dso_whitelist:
        - $RPATH/libcuda.so.*
    script: install_tests.sh
    requirements:
      build:
        - {{ compiler('c') }}
        - {{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - {{ compiler('cuda') }}
        - {{ stdlib('c') }}
        - arm-variant * {{ arm_variant_type }}  # [linux and aarch64 and cuda_compiler_version != "None"]
        - cmake >=3.20.1
      host:
        - cuda-version {{ cuda_compiler_version }}
        - cuda-cudart-dev
        - {{ pin_subpackage('libcvcuda', exact=True) }}
        - gtest
        - openssl
      run:
        - {{ pin_compatible('cuda-version', max_pin='x', min_pin='x') }}
        - {{ pin_subpackage('libcvcuda', exact=True) }}
        - cuda-cudart
    test:
      commands:
        - test -f $PREFIX/bin/cvcuda_test_system
        - test -f $PREFIX/bin/cvcuda_test_system_smoke
        - test -f $PREFIX/bin/run_libcvcuda_tests.sh
    about:
      home: https://github.com/CVCUDA/CV-CUDA
      summary: 'C++ system tests for CV-CUDA operators'
      license: Apache-2.0 and MIT
      license_file:
        - LICENSE.md
        - THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES.md
      doc_url: https://cvcuda.github.io/CV-CUDA/
      dev_url: https://github.com/CVCUDA/CV-CUDA

about:
  home: https://github.com/CVCUDA/CV-CUDA
  summary: 'An open-source, GPU accelerated library for cloud-scale image processing and computer vision'
  description: |
    CV-CUDA is an open-source library of GPU-accelerated computer vision algorithms designed
    for speed and scalability. It delivers high-throughput, low-latency image/video
    processing for AI pipelines across NVIDIA cloud, desktop, and edge platforms. CV-CUDA is
    built for performance and works seamlessly with C/C++ and Python Image and AI
    frameworks.

    This package provides the C/C++ shared libraries and their headers.
  license: Apache-2.0 and MIT
  license_file:
    - LICENSE.md
    - THIRD_PARTY_LICENSES.md
  doc_url: https://cvcuda.github.io/CV-CUDA/
  dev_url: https://github.com/CVCUDA/CV-CUDA

extra:
  feedstock-name: libcvcuda
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/cuda
